data.title: Chinese automakers pressure the Japanese market with lower prices
data.description:
Chinese automakers are exerting increasing influence in the Japanese auto market, particularly in the compact-car segment where domestic brands have long been dominant. The entry of Chinese manufacturers such as BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF), Leapmotor, NIO (NIO), and Geely (OTCPK:GELYF) has coincided with a sharp decline in electric vehicle prices driven by fierce competition within China and excess production capacity.

The Guangzhou International Automobile Exhibition exemplified the trend, with several Chinese carmakers unveiling budget-friendly models planned for overseas sales, some priced as low as 100,000 yuan ($14,000). "Our mission is to make this car our flagship model for drivers all over the world," stated Leapmotor Vice President Cao Li.

The growing presence of Chinese EVs in Japan is having ripple effects across the industry, with Japanese automakers being forced to accelerate their electrification plans, as seen in Honda's (HMC) introduction of six new electric models and Suzuki's (OTCPK:SZKMY) push into the compact EV market. Suzuki (OTCPK:SZKMY) executives recently publicly acknowledged that BYD's (OTCPK:BYDDF) entry is acting as a catalyst for the growing popularity of small EVs in Japan, prompting domestic brands to rethink their product strategies and speed up new launches to stay competitive.

Notably, Chinese automakers are tailoring their vehicles to local Japanese preferences and investing in dealer networks and fast-charging infrastructure to overcome barriers like brand loyalty and range anxiety.

Despite the recent EV momentum, battery and plug-in vehicles account for a single-digit percent of new car sales in Japan. Yet, analysts think Chinese EVs could accelerate the transition, as their lower price points and advanced features address concerns held by price-sensitive commuters and second-car buyers. Additionally, some Chinese models are being adapted for Japan’s right-hand-drive requirements, making them more accessible for local consumers.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Alibaba earnings, AI anxiety, airline M&A, and a Burry teaser
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - November 24

  • Volatility watch - Oklo (OKLO) and Strategy (MSTR) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Exact Sciences (EXAS), Blue Acquisition (BACC), and Magnitude International (MAGH). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Perrigo (PRGO), Greystone Housing Impact Investors (GHI), and Janus International (JBI). Sonnet BioTherapeutics (SONN) and Mobileye (MBLY) head into the week with a higher level of short interest outstanding than in the past.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Hyatt (H), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), T-Mobile US (TMUS), and eBay (EBAY).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Agilent Technologies (A), Zoom Communications (ZM), and Symbotic (SYM).
  • All day - The TD Cowen Treatment Advancements in Obesity & Related Disorders Summit will take place. Participating companies include MBX Biosciences (MBX) and Aardvark Therapeutics (AARD).
  • 8:00 a.m. Canadian National Railway (CNI) CFO Ghislain Houle will address the Desjardins Toronto Conference.
  • 9:00 a.m. The changes from the MSCI Quarterly Index Review will take place. The largest additions to the MSCI World Index include CoreWeave (CRWV), Nebius Group (NBIS), and Insmed (INSM).

 

Tuesday - November 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Alibaba (BABA), Analog Devices (ADI), Dell Technologies (DELL), Best Buy (BBY), Autodesk (ADSK), Workday (WDAY), Zscaler (ZS), HP (HPQ), Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), J.M. Smucker (SJM), and NIO (NIO). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Kohl's (KSS) and Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) after they report.
  • All day - Michael Burry has teased about a disclosure or release of some sort occurring during the day. There is speculation it could concern new warnings from the famed "The Big Short investor" about the U.S. stock market, particularly regarding the artificial intelligence boom and accounting practices of major tech firms such as Nvidia (NVDA), Oracle (ORCL), Meta (META), and Palantir (PLTR).
  • All day - Mesa Air's (MESA) merger with Republic Airways (RJET) is expected to close. The combination will create one of the largest U.S. regional airline companies.
  • All day - Shareholders of Hanesbrands (HBI) will vote on the proposed merger with Gildan Activewear (GIL).
  • All day - The Nevada Gaming Control Board is expected to release gaming revenue numbers for October. Analysts will be watching the Las Vegas Strip numbers closely amid tourism concerns.
  • 8:00 a.m. Best Buy (BBY) will hold its earnings conference call and give its outlook for the holiday quarter. Suppliers Netgear (NTGR), Turtle Beach (TBCH), and Sonos (SONO) all generate more than 10% of their revenue from Best Buy and have a high correlation to it on earnings day.
  • 8:30 a.m. The delayed October Retail Sales report is expected to be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
  • 4:30 p.m. Dell Technologies (DELL) will hold a highly anticipated earnings conference call. Options trading implies a 10% swing for shares following the earnings release. Intel (INTC) will be on watch, since it has traded in the same direction as Dell after its last eight earnings reports.
  • 4:30 p.m. Nasdaq will release its latest update on short interest positions.

 

Wednesday - November 26

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Deere (DE) and Li Auto (LI).
  • All day - Starbucks (SBUX) and Costa Coffee (KO) are two of the notable companies participating in the European Coffee Symposium in Berlin, which is considered the most influential coffee industry conference on the continent.
  • 8:00 a.m. Adobe will release season-to-date holiday sales data just ahead of the key Black Friday to Cyber Monday period.
  • 8:00 a.m. The delayed October Durable Goods Orders will be released.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.

 

Thursday - November 27

  • All day - The U.S. stock market will be closed for observance of the Thanksgiving holiday.
  • All day - Daimler Truck (OTCPK:DTRUY) will be one of the companies presenting at the Jefferies Paris Industrials Day event.

 

Friday - November 28

  • Oil watch - The energy sector will be in the spotlight ahead of the OPEC+ alliance's major meeting scheduled for November 30, where both the full OPEC+ group and an eight-member subgroup will reassess oil production strategy amid evolving market conditions. OPEC+ is expected to review and potentially re-establish quotas for each member country with a focus on maintaining internal group cohesion, monitoring conformity, and reassessing the compensation mechanisms for any past overproduction.
  • All day - The U.S. stock market will close at 1:00 p.m.
  • All day - It is the FDA action date for Otsuka's (OTCPK:OTSKY) sibeprenlimab.
  • 5:00 p.m. Salesforce will release sales data on Black Friday shopping.

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article.

data.title: WillScot upgraded at Baird as 'watershed' Q3 establishes 'investable bottom'
data.description:
WillScot (WSC) +12.3% in Friday's trading as Baird upgraded North America's largest provider of modular space and portable storage rental solutions to Outperform from Market Perform with a $22 price target, raised from $20, seeing Q3 as "a watershed quarter" that significantly lowers expectations and establishes what could be an "investable bottom."

Baird analyst Andrew Wittmann sees WillScot (WSC) as an attractive long-term opportunity, led by leading market position, secular growth of value added products and services, with ample free cash flow likely to be returned liberally to shareholders.

WillScot's (WSC) impressive unit economics generally offer 25%-plus internal rates of return and a degree of secular revenue opportunity through increasing the value added products and services content rented to customers; as a result, it has a degree of "embedded" revenue growth on its nearly three-year-long average rental period, Wittman said.

The analyst said patient investors can clip a mid-to-high teens free cash flow yield at "bottoming" expectations while waiting for future rate cuts to improve non-residential cycle rotation into cyclical growth, which may take time, "but deeply negative sentiment combined with high operational/financial leverage should lead to strong stock resurgence once cycle improves."

data.title: AstraZeneca expands U.S. production footprint with $2B investment in Maryland
data.description:
AstraZeneca (AZN) on Friday announced plans to invest $2B in Maryland to boost its U.S. production capacity with a sizable expansion of an existing biologics facility in Frederick and a new clinical manufacturing site in Gaithersburg.

As part of the plans, the Anglo-Swedish drugmaker said that it will nearly double the manufacturing capacity of its Frederick facility, enabling the site to manufacture rare disease products for the first time.

Astra (AZN) added that concurrently it will construct the new Gaithersburg site to ensure a continuous supply of novel molecules for use in clinical studies.

While the Frederick facility is expected to generate 200 highly skilled positions and 900 construction jobs for the U.S. economy, the Gaithersburg site will add 100 skilled jobs and 1,000 construction-related jobs and retain 400 additional roles, the company said.

Both initiatives are set to become operational in 2029 as part of Astra’s (AZN) plans to invest $50B in the U.S. by the end of the decade.

data.title: Nasdaq rallies 2% after the prior session’s wild whiplash
data.description:
Wall Street rebounded higher on Friday, a day after the Nasdaq Composite saw a dramatic session due to a tech-led sell-off.

The current market boost has been fueled by reports that the Trump administration is considering allowing the sale of H200 GPUs to China.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last up by 2%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) had pushed up by 1.8% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) advanced by 1.7%. 

From a sector-by-sector point of view, all of the 11 S&P segments are tracking higher in the green and are led by Communication Services. At the other end of the spectrum, Utilities has been the worst performer on the session so far. 

Treasury yields ticked lower as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved down by 1 basis point to 4.07% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed down by 1 basis point to 3.52%. 

Market participants are also still digesting a turbulent session on Thursday that sent the Nasdaq Composite on a dramatic round trip—from up more than 2% in the morning to down over 2% at the close.

“While we expect volatility in 2026, we do expect a positive return with the S&P 500 reaching 7500 by the end of next year,” Mary Ann Bartels, chief investment strategist at Sanctuary Wealth said. 

“As we enter 2026, the equity market has had great performance since the lows of the bear market in 2022. We expect 2026 to be the reset year for the leadership of the market in the big Growth Technology stocks. We believe this reset will position stocks to continue their leadership into the end of the decade. Despite this anticipated correction in the large cap area of the market, we don’t see leadership emerging from small caps,” Mary Ann Bartels added. 

On the economic front, there are a few Federal Reserve speakers lined up, including Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan and Boston Fed President Susan Collins. 

The S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI rose slightly to 54.8 in November from 54.6 in the prior month. At the same time, Services PMI came in at 55 and manufacturing PMI came in at 51.9.

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of Intuit Inc (INTU) are higher by 6%, while shares of Oracle (ORCL) have slid lower by 4.8%.

data.title: Nvidia shares erase losses as Trump floats idea of selling H200 GPUs to China: report
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) shares erased their losses on Friday and turned higher after Bloomberg reported the Trump Administration has floated the idea of selling H200 GPUs to China.

Officials are still in the early stages of the discussion, and no final decision on the matter has been made, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. 

An Nvidia spokesperson told Seeking Alpha that, “the regulatory landscape does not allow us to offer a competitive datacenter GPU in China, leaving that massive market to our rapidly growing foreign competitors. Our foreclosure from the China datacenter compute market has no impact on our ability to supply customers in the U.S.A.”

The discussions come amid a contentious time for the Trump Administration as it pertains to artificial intelligence regulations and letting companies such as Nvidia, AMD (AMD) and others to supply chips to competitor nations, such as China.

A bipartisan group of U.S. lawmakers unveiled a bill in the House on Thursday that would restrict the purchase of Chinese chipmaking equipment by CHIPS Act grant recipients for 10 years. Nvidia did not receive money directly from the bipartisan CHIPS Act that was passed under President Biden, but its major manufacturing partner, Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), did.

In July, after months of back and forth talks with the U.S. government, Nvidia said it had received U.S. approval to sell a slimmed down version of its Hopper line of GPUs, the H20, to China. In its most recent quarter, Nvidia did not record any H20 revenue from China. The previous quarter did not see any H20 sales to China-based customers, but Nividia did benefit from a $180M release of previously reserved H20 inventory, from approximately $650M in unrestricted H20 sales to a customer outside of China.

Earlier this month, it was reported that the Chinese government had banned the use of foreign made AI chips in its state-funded data centers. The Chinese government has also previously told local firms to stop buying Nvidia's AI GPUs.

In October, President Trump said he had not discussed the sale of Nvidia's most powerful line of AI accelerators, its Blackwell series, during his meeting with Chinese Premier Xi Jinping.

The White House and U.S. Commerce Department did not immediately respond to requests for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Helmerich & Payne upgraded, Atlas Energy cut to Sell at Goldman
data.description:
Helmerich & Payne (HP) +3.3% in Friday's trading as Goldman Sachs upgraded the oil and gas drilling services provider to Neutral from Sell with a $28 price target, while Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) -4.5% as the bank downgraded shares to Sell from Neutral with an $8 PT, driven by its outlook of the macro rate of change for each stock.

For H&P (HP), Goldman analyst Ati Modak said the exposure to Saudi activity improvements in 2026 along with further upside from Oman and Algeria should create positive rate of change for rig activation throughout the year along with an improving balance sheet in a relatively more stable North America backdrop.

The analyst slightly raised his EBIDTA estimates slightly for H&P to reflect the company's increased international activity exposure and continued focus on deleveraging.

For Atlas Energy (AESI), Modak said he remains cautious about 2026 with lower sand prices Y/Y in the $18-$19/ton range, a small improvement in volumes Y/Y with ~21.7 million tons driven by market share gains in a challenging backdrop vs. ~21.2 million tons in 2025, and challenging logistics profitability, resulting in the analyst's forecast for EBITDA estimates ~10% below FactSet consensus.

Modak said Atlas Energy's (AESI) idiosyncratic strengths in market share gains and full utilization on the Dune Express should allow for better performance than other sand peers, but the macro remains challenged relative to these strengths.

data.title: Auna outlines $500M Mexico expansion and targets out-of-pocket revenue growth amid operational turnaround
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Auna S.A. (AUNA) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Jesús Zamora Leon, President, CEO & Executive Chairman of the Board, opened by stating, "In this quarter, we're reporting weaker financial results, a flat quarter principally dragged down by our Mexican operations. However, in Mexico, we are seeing evidence of stable and growing operational activity, the strength of our business model, the stage of development of our operations and the resilience of our owners integrated regional platform were reflected in the strong results of our Peruvian and Colombian segment in the third quarter, which partially offset the 5% decline in total adjusted EBITDA that was the result of Mexico's performance."
  • The CEO highlighted that Peru and Colombia contributed to a 1% increase in FX-neutral consolidated revenue, with Peru's top line in local currency growing 9% and Colombia's by 4%.
  • Zamora Leon outlined a strategic focus in Mexico, noting, "We have revamped the leadership team in Mexico" and detailed new appointments including Alejandro Torres as head of Monterrey operations and a new Chief Medical Officer. The company is also "rolling out a series of package service offering and strengthening our collaboration with leading physicians to further penetrate 3 important market segments," with a target to increase the Out-of-Pocket segment from 8% to 20% of Mexico revenue by the end of next year.
  • The CEO reported, "Oncology and cardiology services, which are integral to our long-term growth strategy, increased 48% versus second quarter 2025, accounting for 15% of Mexico's revenues."
  • Zamora Leon also announced, "It is also important to note our recent public announcement on our partnership with Sojitz. This will allow us to accelerate growth in Mexico beyond what we can achieve on our own, while maintaining our disciplined deleveraging path and our target of bringing leverage below 3x."
  • Gisele Ferrero, CFO & Executive VP, stated, "Peru still accounted for well over half of our platform's third quarter and year-to-date revenues. And with its top line increasing 9% in the quarter, Peru continues being a strong and reliable driver of growth and cash flow."
  • Ferrero also noted, "Peru was also a strong contributor of adjusted EBITDA growth at 15% growth. It also had a solid margin of 22.7% in the quarter."

Outlook

  • Zamora Leon expressed, "We anticipate 2026 to be a year of full recovery in Mexico, and with the New Mexico team in place, we remain very bullish in the medium term."
  • Management expects Mexico's revenue to "begin growing again next year."
  • Zamora Leon forecast, "2025 will be a flat year. 2026 will be a growth year. And definitely, it will be a growth year in Mexico."
  • The company aims to increase the Out-of-Pocket segment in Mexico from 8% to 20% by the end of 2026 and highlighted a $500 million investment plan for Mexico over the next 3 to 5 years, in partnership with Sojitz.

Financial Results

  • Zamora Leon reported, "Our adjusted net income was a solid PEN 58 million for the quarter."
  • Ferrero detailed, "Our pretax operating cash flow decreased 5% to PEN 595 million during the 9-month period, mainly due to lower revenues in Mexico and accounts receivable delays related to the systems integration there. Compared to the second quarter of this year, our pretax operating cash flow increased 65% sequentially with improved collections and cash conversion in Colombia."
  • The company ended the quarter with a cash position of PEN 226 million, a 4.2% decrease from the beginning of the year.
  • Auna completed a $765 million debt refinancing, including a $365 million senior secured notes issuance and a $400 million equivalent term loan in Mexican pesos, which the CFO stated "represent 125 basis points in savings versus the interest rates on the previous debt structure."

Q&A

  • Mauricio Cepeda, Morgan Stanley, Research Division: Asked about the rationale for expanding in Mexico and the MoU with Sojitz, as well as the outlook for EPS in Colombia. Zamora Leon responded, "Today, leverage is a key concern, and we want to bring it below 3x... we have found with Sojitz a really interesting opportunity... The MoU we negotiated during the last few months formalizes our relationship and puts up a framework to co-invest together in Mexico."
  • Joseph Giordano, JPMorgan: Inquired about the partnership with Sojitz and the Trecca project. Zamora Leon explained, "Trecca is a public-private partnership awarded to Auna back in 2010... This contract will deliver certainly sales of over $200 million a year when it scales."
  • Multiple questions addressed the share price decline and investment case. Zamora Leon asserted, "We are convinced the current prices now reflect the company's fundamentals. There has been no significant change from what we've been reporting to the company's fundamentals."
  • Further queries covered return on investment timelines, expansion plans, bed additions, and the structure of the $500 million Mexico investment, all reaffirming management's focus on measured, debt-neutral growth and operational recovery.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on the Mexico turnaround timeline, partnership with Sojitz, and the share price decline, reflecting a slightly negative to neutral tone, with repeated questions about capital allocation and strategic priorities.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone in prepared remarks and during Q&A, reiterating belief in the underlying business and future growth. Zamora Leon emphasized, "Auna's diversified footprint and integrated model provides enduring resilience," and repeatedly stated confidence in the recovery and growth trajectory for Mexico and the group as a whole.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone was more pointed regarding Mexico’s underperformance and share price, while management, though candid about challenges, expressed greater urgency and detail in outlining turnaround and growth initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to explicitly describe 2025 as a flat year and 2026 as a growth year, compared to prior general optimism.
  • The strategic focus narrowed further on Mexico's turnaround, leadership changes, and out-of-pocket revenue growth, whereas last quarter's focus was more on overall stabilization and efficiency.
  • Analysts this quarter emphasized concerns about share price and capital structure, while last quarter’s questions were broader, spanning tax rates and market share.
  • Key metrics this quarter highlighted the PEN 58 million adjusted net income, 5% decline in consolidated adjusted EBITDA, and a 4.2% decrease in cash position, compared to prior quarter’s focus on EBITDA growth and market share in Monterrey.
  • Management’s tone evolved from cautious optimism to direct acknowledgment of operational setbacks but with a detailed action plan for recovery and renewed growth, especially in Mexico.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited operational challenges in Mexico, including slower-than-expected recovery in doctor volumes, impacts from new hospital information and ERP systems, and a higher mix of lower-margin services.
  • The company is addressing these through IT upgrades, targeted recruitment, and enhanced collaboration with physicians.
  • Analysts raised concerns over share price performance, return on investment, and the execution timeline for Mexico’s recovery.

Final Takeaway

Auna’s third quarter results reflected operational headwinds in Mexico that weighed on consolidated performance, but management is advancing a robust turnaround strategy anchored in new leadership, targeted service expansion, and a partnership with Sojitz to drive a $500 million investment plan. Despite short-term financial softness, the group remains optimistic about a full recovery in Mexico by 2026, with strong contributions continuing from Peru and Colombia, and an explicit commitment to strengthening the balance sheet and enhancing shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Roku poised to end in green after seven straight sessions of declines
data.description:
Roku (ROKU) was on track to snap seven straight sessions of losses on Friday after shares climbed over 3% during mid-day trading. Shares of the streaming platform rose as much as 3.17% to $93.87.

The company began its downward course on November 12. The following day, on November 13, its shares closed 5.58% lower at $100.01. Between Nov 12 and Nov 20, it declined by over 14%.

As per Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, the stock has a Hold rating with a score of 3.39 out of 5. ROKU has been rated a D- for valuation, but it has been graded an A+ for growth.

However, Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts have issued a Buy call for the stock.

Earlier this month, Seeking Alpha analyst Stone Fox Capital reiterated its Strong Buy rating for Roku following its third-quarter results.

With platform revenues rising 17%, outpacing user engagement growth and monetization initiatives expected to drive higher ARPU and revenue, the analyst believes that the company is positioned for strong growth.

It continues to find new ways to convert its sizeable user base into steady growth, while rising profits give it greater flexibility to deploy its large cash reserves- whether to reinvest in the business or ramp up share buybacks, Stone Fox Capital noted.

“Investors should use any retest of $100 as an opportunity to buy the streaming platform stock on weakness in front of a multi-year growth trajectory,” they additionally said.

The company reported its third-quarter results in October, where it reported a profit beat and guided the fourth quarter slightly ahead of analysts’ estimates.

Despite its recent fall, ROKU has gained over 25% year-to-date, outpacing the broader markets, which rose nearly 12%. In the past year, the stock has advanced by over 32%.

data.title: These are the impacts of the markets ‘unwinding’ their 2025 ‘U.S. risk premium’ – GS
data.description:
The U.S. market (SP500), (COMP:IND), (DJI), (US10Y), (US2Y) has largely unwound its earlier concerns about structural risks that drove unusual trading patterns in early 2025, according to Vickie Chang, macro strategist in Goldman Sachs Research.

“The market looks to have sharply increased its estimation of the risk premium in U.S. assets in the wake of the reciprocal tariff announcement at the beginning of April, but has since largely unwound that move,” she said, noting that since late July, the pattern has shifted to “continued compression of that increased U.S. risk premium.”

 

 

 

Earlier this year, markets exhibited an unusual pattern where the U.S. dollar (DXY) weakened alongside U.S. equity (SP500), (COMP:IND), (DJI) and bond (US10Y), (US5Y), (US2Y) weakness, differing from standard monetary policy tightening shocks.

This correlation pattern is “consistent with what would be expected if the market demanded an increased risk premium for U.S. assets,” the strategist explained.

Several specific risks dominated market concerns in April and May, including tariff risks, fiscal concerns, and worries about Fed independence prompted by speculation about potential leadership changes. “In each case, the deeper tail risks have receded relative to that earlier period,” Chang said, while warning that “with the market having largely reversed its earlier fears, it would now be more vulnerable to even more modest challenges on these structural risks.”

These concerns could resurface, particularly around the expected Fed chair announcement in December or January. “Worries that the Fed might be easing too aggressively into a more robust growth or inflation backdrop could also fuel fears about these risks,” she said.

Similarly, “focus on fiscal risks could resurface too if tariff policies are constrained by the Supreme Court or fresh tax rebates are more seriously considered.”

If U.S. structural risks do reemerge, the market reaction could include “lower front-end yields and higher back-end yields, and so a steeper curve; a weaker U.S. dollar (DXY) particularly against reserve and safe-haven currencies; and higher gold (XAUUSD:CUR) prices,” Chang concluded.

However, she noted an alternative scenario: “If the Fed chair appointment reassures investors, this could lead the market to reduce its weight on these risks further,” potentially putting “upward pressure on U.S. front-end yields and potentially some support for the USD.”
